entries.title: First Telegraph Message
entries.headline: Samuel Morse sent the first long-distance telegraph message from Washington to Baltimore: "What hath God wrought."
entries.text: Samuel Morse sent the first long-distance telegraph message from Washington, D.C., to Baltimore, turning electricity into a practical communications system and shrinking political, financial, and social time.
entries.dek: Why it mattered: A communications breakthrough that helped make the modern world faster and more connected.

facts.label: Why it mattered

summary: On May 24, 1844, Samuel Morse sent the first long-distance telegraph message from Washington, D.C., to Baltimore, turning electricity into a practical communications system and shrinking political, financial, and social time.
